title: Wear OS 7 will keep track of deliveries and sports scores on your wrist
summary: Google announced Wear OS 7 with Live Updates for deliveries and sports scores on smartwatches, plus new Wear Widgets offering more customizable watch experiences similar to Android widgets.
content:
A Pixel Watch 4 running Wear OS 6, not Wear OS 7. | Photo by Amelia Holowaty Krales / The Verge	

Amid the flurry of today's Google I/O announcements, Google shared details about Wear OS 7, the next major update to its smartwatch platform. To help you keep track of things like deliveries and sports scores, Wear OS 7 will get the iPhone-style Live Updates that were introduced on Android last year - which can appear on your watch or your smartphone - and you'll also be able to track automated tasks that an AI is working on right from your watch.
Wear OS is also getting an upgrade from its widget-like Tiles for glanceable information. With the new update, Google is adding "Wear Widgets" to the platform, which look more like Android widgets …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: Wear OS 7 将在你的手腕上跟踪包裹和体育比分
zh.summary: 谷歌宣布推出 Wear OS 7，在智能手表上提供包裹和体育比分的实时更新，并推出新的 Wear Widgets，提供与 Android 小部件类似的更多自定义手表体验。

title: Plex is tripling the price of a lifetime pass to $750 after doubling it last year
summary: Plex is raising lifetime subscription prices to $750 by July 1st, following a doubling from $119.99 to $249.99 last year, prompting customers to lock in current rates before the increase.
content:
I am dying to know how much money Plex is about to make the next six weeks charging people to stream their own video from their own homes. Today, it's giving every prospective customer until July 1st to lock in a lifetime subscription at today's rates - before it triples the price to $750. 
Plex already more than doubled the price of a lifetime Plex Pass subscription from $119.99 to $249.99 last March, and it'll triple again on July 1st to $749.99. At that price, you'd have to subscribe for 11 years (at the current annual rate) to make the lifetime sub worthwhile.
Plex isn't choosing the new number because it expects you to pay $750. In a  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: Plex 在去年价格翻倍后，将终身通行证的价格提高至 750 美元
zh.summary: Plex 宣布从 7 月 1 日起将终身订阅价格提高至 750 美元，在去年从 119.99 美元翻倍至 249.99 美元之后，促使客户在涨价前锁定当前费率。

title: Demis Hassabis said this might be the ‘foothills of the singularity.’ What?
summary: Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis described current AI developments as standing "in the foothills of the singularity," framing advanced AI as a transformative force for scientific discovery and human progress.
content:
Welcome to a "profound moment for humanity," according to Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis, who closed out Google I/O's keynote presentation on Tuesday, saying:
Google's cutting-edge research and products will help unlock AGI's incredible potential for the benefit of the entire world. When we look back at this time, I think we will realize that we were standing in the foothills of the singularity. 
It will be a profound moment for humanity. This technology will be a force multiplier for human ingenuity and usher in a new golden age of scientific discovery and progress, improving the lives of everyone, everywhere. We look forward to bu …

Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: Demis Hassabis 说这可能是"奇点的山麓"。什么？
zh.summary: 谷歌 DeepMind 首席执行官 Demis Hassabis 将当前的人工智能发展描述为站在"奇点的山麓"，将先进的人工智能视为科学发现和人类进步的变革力量。

title: We react to Google I/O 2026
summary: The Vergecast discusses Google I/O 2026's major announcements including AI agents, Gmail improvements, and industry implications of Google's shift toward agent-based solutions.
content:
What better way to unwind from a two-hour keynote presentation than to pore over the weirdest and wildest details, from a Gmail bot you can converse with to DeepMind's leader saying the singularity is near. The Vergecast went live right after the show, with senior AI reporter Hayden Field joining me to discuss the highlights.
First up, we talk about the buzzword of the show: agents, which seem to be Google's new answer to making AI useful. Google has a new service called Spark that sounds a lot like a Gemini-flavored OpenClaw, upgrades to its Antigravity platform, and a bunch of agents that will shop for you and track search topics. We also …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.summary: Vergecast讨论了Google I/O 2026的主要公告，包括AI代理、Gmail改进以及Google向基于代理解决方案转变的行业影响。

title: The future of Google is a search box that does everything
summary: Google is reimagining its search platform with dynamic query expansion and AI-powered suggestions that go beyond traditional autocomplete, creating a more comprehensive information assistant.
content:
Last year, after watching Google's I/O keynote, I wrote that it felt like Google's future was Google googling. After watching this year's I/O keynote on Tuesday, I don't think Google just wants to google for you - I think it wants to do everything for you, all from a search box.
Take the trusty Google search bar itself, something Google is generally hesitant to update, which is getting some updates. It will "dynamically" expand as you type longer queries. It will offer "AI-powered suggestions" that Google claims will "go beyond autocomplete," which could cause you to fill in the blanks of a search in a way you didn't intend and that may or  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

content: Good news! Discord's hundreds of millions of users now have their communications scrambled, so not even Discord can see them.

content: Ocean, an agentic email security platform, claims its AI can thoroughly analyze the context of every incoming email to detect fraud and impersonation attempts.

title: Nintendo’s $500 Switch 2 bundle includes a game, and it’s available now
summary: Nintendo's Switch 2 "Choose Your Game" bundle is available at major retailers for $499.99, allowing consumers to select one of three digital games before prices increase in September.
content:
Mario Kart World is one of three games you can choose from in the bundle. | Photo by Amelia Holowaty Krales / The Verge	

Nintendo recently teased the “Choose Your Game” Switch 2 console and digital game bundle, coming in early June. However, multiple retailers (including Nintendo itself) are already offering the $499.99 bundle. The list currently includes GameStop, Amazon, Best Buy, and the Nintendo Store. It’s a deal worth hopping on ahead of the holiday season, since Nintendo is raising the price of the Switch 2 to $499.99 in September.
While the Switch 2 console currently costs $449.99 by itself, the bundle includes a full-price game with up to a $30 discount.  You can choose between one of three digital games to redeem upon setup: Donkey Kong Bananza, Pokémon Pokopia, and Mario Kart World. Each of those are regularly $69.99 except for World, which retails for $79.99. 
Nintendo Switch 2 “Choose Your Game” bundle
Choose between Donkey Kong Bananza, Mario Kart World, and Pokémon Pokopia.

Where to Buy:

 $529.99 $499.99 at Amazon
 $529.99 $499.99 at Best Buy
 $529.99 $499.99 at GameStop

None of the games offered as part of the bundle will take up much of the Switch 2’s 256GB internal SSD. Mario Kart World takes up the most space of the three, requiring 22GB. However, you may eventually own enough games to exceed its capacity, so we recommend considering a 256GB Samsung microSD Express card to double your available storage. There’s currently a deal on those happening at Amazon, where you can buy one for $47.99 (originally $79.99).
To redeem your game, enter the eShop from the Switch 2’s home screen. Then select “enter code” on the left side of the page. You’ll then enter the 16-character code provided in your bundle, then make your game selection.

zh.title: 任天堂500美元Switch 2套餐包含游戏，现已上市
zh.summary: 任天堂Switch 2"选择你的游戏"套餐在主要零售商以$499.99的价格出售，消费者可在9月前选择三款数字游戏之一，之后价格将上涨。

title: Google’s AI future demands trust — and your personal data
summary: Google's new AI tools like Gemini Spark and Daily Brief promise productivity benefits but rely heavily on personal data access, raising important questions about user trust and privacy.
content:
Google has big promises for its AI-powered future - and a lot of it depends on your trust. At I/O 2026, Google described a bunch of new tools that it claims will make your life easier. Gemini Spark, Google's always-on AI agent, can help organize an upcoming event, while Daily Brief can offer a rundown of what to expect during your day. Google is even expanding access to Gmail's AI inbox, which can generate custom to-do lists and draft personalized replies based on your emails.
Many of these features seem genuinely useful, but at the heart of each of them is an AI engine that runs on a trove of personal information. While other AI companies, …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 谷歌人工智能未来需要信任——以及你的个人数据
zh.summary: 谷歌的新人工智能工具（如Gemini Spark和Daily Brief）承诺提高生产力，但严重依赖个人数据访问权限，引发了关于用户信任和隐私的重要问题。

title: RFK Jr. forced to withdraw charter that opened CDC panel to anti-vaccine quacks
summary: RFK Jr. was forced to withdraw a charter that would have expanded CDC panel membership eligibility and focused on alleged vaccine injuries. The decision prevented anti-vaccine advocates from gaining positions within the federal health agency. This reflects ongoing institutional concerns about vaccine skepticism influencing public health policy.
content: Charter would have expanded member eligibility and focused on alleged injuries.

zh.summary: 小肯尼迪被迫撤销了一项特许权,该特许权原本会扩大美国疾病控制与预防中心(CDC)委员会的成员资格范围,并关注所谓的疫苗伤害。这一决定阻止了反疫苗倡导者进入联邦卫生机构内部。这反映了人们对疫苗怀疑主义影响公共卫生政策的持续担忧。

title: Google announces agent-optimized Gemini 3.5.Flash and a do-anything model called Omni
summary: Google announced Gemini 3.5 Flash, an optimized model designed for autonomous AI agent applications, and a versatile "do-anything" model called Omni. The company positions Gemini 3.5 Flash as essential infrastructure for the emerging agentic AI market. These releases represent Google's competitive strategy in the rapidly evolving AI agent space.
content: Google says its more efficient Gemini 3.5 Flash is the key to your agentic AI future.

title: Google’s Genie world model can now simulate real streets with Street View
summary: Google DeepMind integrated Street View data with Project Genie to create immersive, interactive world simulations featuring real-world environments, weather variations, and rare scenarios. The technology enables applications in robotics, gaming, and travel planning. This advancement demonstrates AI-generated world models' potential for realistic environmental simulation.
content: Google DeepMind is integrating Street View with Project Genie to create immersive, interactive world simulations for robotics, gaming, and travel, allowing users to explore environments, weather changes, and rare scenarios.

zh.title: 谷歌Genie世界模型现已支持利用街景数据模拟真实街道
zh.summary: 谷歌DeepMind将街景数据与Project Genie整合,创建了具有真实世界环境、天气变化和稀有场景的沉浸式交互式世界模拟。该技术可应用于机器人、游戏和旅游规划领域。这一进展展示了AI生成世界模型在逼真环境模拟中的巨大潜力。

The release signals a strategic shift from chatbot-focused AI toward autonomous agent systems. This positions agentic AI as the next major computing paradigm.
content: Google launched Gemini 3.5 Flash, its most powerful coding and agentic AI model yet, at the company's annual developer conference. It is capable of autonomously executing complex tasks and building software from scratch.

zh.title: 用Gemini 3.5 Flash，谷歌押注下一波AI浪潮在智能体上，而非聊天机器人

The agents automate the labor-intensive process of tracking information sources and curating alerts. This represents a shift toward intelligent, always-running AI assistants.

title: Google Search as you know it is over
summary: Google is transforming its search engine from a traditional link-based model into an AI-powered experience featuring conversational answers, autonomous agents, and interactive interfaces. The shift could significantly reduce web traffic to publishers dependent on search referrals. This restructuring fundamentally changes how users access and discover online information.
content: Google is transforming Search from a list of links into an AI-powered experience filled with conversational answers, autonomous agents, and interactive interfaces — a shift that could further reduce traffic to publishers across the web.

The release demonstrates Google's embrace of AI-driven development practices. This approach enables third-party interoperability with leading AI coding platforms.
content: Google is embracing the rise of AI coding agents with new Android tools designed to work with platforms like Claude Code and OpenAI’s Codex, allowing developers — or their AI assistants — to build Android apps faster from the command line.

zh.summary: 谷歌发布了Gemini Spark，一款基于Gemini基础模型、由Google Antigravity驱动的24/7智能代理个人助手，支持Gmail集成。该助手可自动化邮件管理和日程安排等日常个人任务。此次发布展示了谷歌对面向消费者的智能代理AI应用的承诺。

title: Google adds voice-based prompting to Docs and Keep
summary: Google added voice-based prompting to Docs and Keep through a new Workspace update, enabling users to create drafts, take notes, and search email using voice commands. The feature enhances accessibility and hands-free productivity. This expansion reflects Google's ongoing integration of AI into its productivity suite.
content: Google is letting users create drafts, take notes, and search for email with voice with the new Workspace update.

zh.title: 谷歌为Docs和Keep添加语音提示功能
zh.summary: 谷歌通过新的Workspace更新为Docs和Keep添加了语音提示功能，使用户能够使用语音命令创建草稿、记笔记和搜索电子邮件。该功能增强了可访问性和免提生产力。此扩展反映了谷歌继续将AI集成到生产力套件中的努力。

title: Google launches Antigravity 2.0 with an updated desktop app and CLI tool
summary: Google launched Antigravity 2.0 with updated desktop and command-line tools alongside a new $100 AI Ultra plan offering five times more usage limits than the existing AI Pro tier. The release demonstrates Google's investment in advanced agentic infrastructure and premium AI services. The pricing strategy targets power users requiring higher computational allowances.
content: Google is debuting a new AI Ultra plan priced at $100, which will give users 5x more usage limit than the  AI Pro plan alongside Antigravity 2.0 launch.

zh.title: 谷歌推出Antigravity 2.0，包含更新的桌面应用和CLI工具
zh.summary: 谷歌推出了Antigravity 2.0，包含更新的桌面和命令行工具，同时推出了新的100美元AI Ultra计划，提供的使用限制比现有AI Pro套餐多五倍。此版本展示了谷歌对先进智能代理基础设施和高级AI服务的投资。定价策略针对需要更高计算额度的高级用户。

title: Nintendo keeps finding new ways to reinvent platformers
summary: Yoshi and the Mysterious Book reimagines the side-scrolling platformer by prioritizing exploration over competition, featuring a storybook aesthetic and a laid-back, playful approach to the genre.
content:
In most platforming games, you're fighting against the world around you. You're trying to beat a level, nail a seemingly impossible series of jumps, or defeat a powerful boss. But even though Yoshi and the Mysterious Book uses familiar gameplay - you traverse the world by jumping, climbing, and, uh, eating - it reframes your goal to focus on exploration instead of competition. And in doing so it reimagines the classic side-scrolling platformer as something that feels refreshingly new: laid-back, playful, and bursting with ideas.
The new Yoshi game looks like a storybook, and that's because it takes place inside of one. Early on you meet a s …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 任天堂不断找到重塑平台游戏的新方式
zh.summary: 《Yoshi与神秘之书》通过优先考虑探索而非竞争，重新想象了横版平台游戏，采用故事书美学和悠闲、趣味盎然的游戏方式。

title: Microsoft launches Surface Pro 12 and Surface Laptop 8 with Intel chips
summary: Microsoft releases Surface Pro 12 and Surface Laptop 8 with Intel Core Ultra Series 3 processors for business customers, starting at $1,949.99, before similar Snapdragon X2 models arrive later this year.
content:
The new Surface Laptop 8 and Surface Pro 12 models. | Image: Microsoft	

Almost exactly two years ago, Microsoft introduced Arm-powered versions of the Surface Pro 11 and Surface Laptop 7, with Intel models not ready until more than six months later. This time around Microsoft is refreshing its Surface Pro and Surface Laptop models with Intel's latest Core Ultra Series 3 processors first, ahead of similar models with Qualcomm's new Snapdragon X2 processors later this year.
The new Surface Pro 12, or as Microsoft calls it the Surface Pro for Business 13-inch (12th Edition), will be available for businesses today, starting at an eye-watering $1,949.99. The base model will include an Intel Core Ultra 5 processor, 1 …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 微软推出搭载英特尔芯片的Surface Pro 12和Surface Laptop 8
zh.summary: 微软为商业客户发布了搭载英特尔Core Ultra Series 3处理器的Surface Pro 12和Surface Laptop 8，起价1,949.99美元，之后还会推出类似的Snapdragon X2型号。

title: The Mandalorian and Grogu should have been a season of TV
summary: The Mandalorian's recent seasons have become overstuffed with supporting characters and confusing lore, losing the appeal of its acclaimed first season that refreshed Star Wars storytelling.
content:
When The Mandalorian first debuted on Disney Plus, it was a refreshing reminder of how fascinating Star Wars stories can be when they aren't focused on the same handful of well-established characters. Especially in its first season, the series felt like a sign that Disney was shifting gears after disappointing fans with its last trilogy of big budget features. But as The Mandalorian went on, it became overstuffed with supporting characters and haphazardly-introduced lore that did little to make the show feel like must-see TV.
The relative weakness of The Mandalorian's most recent season is part of what made it so surprising when Lucasfilm a …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 《曼达洛人》和格鲁古应该被制作成一季电视剧
zh.summary: 《曼达洛人》最近几季因充斥过多配角和令人困惑的背景故事而变得臃肿，失去了其备受赞誉的第一季所具有的新鲜《星球大战》故事讲述方式的吸引力。

title: Stilta raises $10.5M from a16z and YC to help companies rediscover the patents they forgot they had
summary: Patent discovery startup Stilta raised $10 million from Andreessen Horowitz and Y Combinator to help companies identify and leverage patents they may have forgotten about.
content: Stilta announced Tuesday a $10 million seed round led by Andreessen Horowitz. Other investors in the round include YC and operators from companies like OpenAI, Legora, and Lovable.

title: Marshall brings ANC back to its smaller on-ear wireless headphones
summary: Marshall launched the Milton A.N.C. wireless on-ear headphones with active noise canceling for $229.99, bringing the feature back to the brand's compact headphone line.
content:
Marshall’s latest headphones carry forward the brand’s distinctive textured leather finish. | ﻿Image: Marshall	

Marshall's first headphones with active noise canceling were the Marshall Mid A.N.C. first launched in 2018. Its current lineup only offers noise canceling on its larger over-ear Monitor III A.N.C. headphones, but its new Milton A.N.C. bring the feature back to Marshall's smaller and lighter on-ear options. The wireless headphones are available today for $229.99 through Marshall's online store, but availability will expand to other "select retailers" starting on May 27th.
That makes the new Milton A.N.C. $70 more expensive than the on-ear Marshall Major V, which have a similar compact design and offer over 100 hours of wireless playtime. Th …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.summary: Marshall 推出了配备主动降噪功能的 Milton A.N.C. 无线耳罩式耳机，价格为 $229.99，该功能回归到该品牌的小型耳机产品线。

title: These are the robot vacuum-mops I recommend for every type of home
summary: The Verge's robot vacuum-mop recommendations are based on six years of real-world testing and prioritize compatibility with your home's layout over specifications, though these devices still require maintenance.
content:
Me and my friends. | Photo: Rose Tuohy / The Verge	

Inviting a robot vacuum-mop into your life is a big decision. An autonomous floor-cleaning machine roaming your home is equal parts glorious and intrusive. But if it does its job well, you may forgive it for eating your rug tassel, running over your Apple Watch, smearing strawberry jam across your carpet, or chattering to you in Chinese at 3AM — all things that have happened to me in my six years of testing these things.
Over that time, I’ve learned that the best robot vacuum-mop is the one most likely to consistently vacuum and mop your floors with the least intervention. And doing that depends far more on your house than on a spec sheet. The first thing I ask someone who wants to buy a robot vacuum is to describe their house. What is your flooring type, how many rugs do you have, what’s the furniture style, are there big room transitions, and how is the home laid out? All these details factor into which robot will work best for you.
Here, I’m going to tell you which robot vacuum-mop I would pick for my home and why, and then go through those I would choose for other common home scenarios — from all-wood floors with the occasional throw rug to a home with wall-to-wall carpet and tile floors. These recommendations aren’t from lab tests or spec sheets; they’re based on actually living with these bots. 
First, let’s set expectations. Robot floor cleaners have improved significantly in the last few years, especially for mopping. Many can now “scrub” your floors instead of just dragging a damp cloth around your house. But they still don’t clean as well as picking up a vacuum and a mop yourself, and they are far from hands-off. 
Most still struggle with corners, baseboards, tight spaces, and dried-on messes, and all of them need maintenance — mopping robots even more so than vacuum-only models. But if these tradeoffs are worth it to you, these robots will keep dirt, dust, and pet hair under control with far less effort

zh.title: 这些是我为每种家庭类型推荐的扫地拖地机器人
zh.summary: The Verge 的扫地拖地机器人推荐基于六年的实际测试，优先考虑与您家庭布局的兼容性而非规格参数，尽管这些设备仍需要维护。

title: Xbox fans want exclusives, more backward compatibility, and free online multiplayer
summary: Xbox players used Microsoft's new Player Voice portal to demand exclusive games, more backward compatible titles, and free online multiplayer, though CEO Asha Sharma has only promised to reevaluate the exclusivity strategy.
content:
Microsoft launched a new Xbox Player Voice portal yesterday, aiming to collect feedback from fans and "make it more visible." It certainly hasn't taken long for Xbox fans to make their feedback very clear. The most upvoted feedback on Xbox Player Voice demands exclusive games for Xbox consoles, more backward compatible games, and free online multiplayer.
Xbox CEO Asha Sharma has already promised that she's "reevaluating" the approach to Xbox-exclusive games and windowed releases of titles, but there has been no firm commitment to reverse the decision to port games to PlayStation and Nintendo Switch. "Xbox was built off of great game exclusi …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: Xbox粉丝要求独占游戏、更多向后兼容游戏和免费在线多人游戏
zh.summary: Xbox玩家利用微软新推出的玩家声音门户网站要求提供独占游戏、更多向后兼容的游戏和免费在线多人游戏，尽管首席执行官阿莎·沙玛仅承诺重新评估独占策略。

title: Google I/O 2026: How to watch and what to expect
summary: Google's I/O developer conference begins May 19th with a keynote presentation showcasing updates to Gemini, Search, and other AI-integrated products, livestreamed on YouTube.
content:
Google's annual developer conference has arrived. We're expecting plenty of updates to Gemini, Search, and every other product that Google has stuffed AI inside of. The keynote kicks off later today - here's what to expect.
When Google I/O will happen and where you can watch it
Google I/O starts at 10AM PT / 1PM ET today, May 19th, with a keynote presentation, which will be livestreamed on Google's YouTube channel and on the Google I/O site. Unlike last week's Android Show, the I/O keynote is usually pretty long, around two hours, so plan accordingly if you're hoping to watch the whole thing. 
The Verge will also be covering the event liv …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: Google I/O 2026：如何观看和期待什么
zh.summary: 谷歌I/O开发者大会将于5月19日开始，主题演讲展示Gemini、搜索和其他AI集成产品的更新，将在YouTube上直播。

title: Gemini is in danger of going full Copilot
summary: Google's Gemini AI is increasingly pervasive across Google apps, drawing comparisons to Microsoft's aggressive Windows 11 Copilot rollout and sparking "AI-everywhere fatigue" among users.
content:
I actually use the Gemini app quite a bit on my phone, but let’s not get carried away.	

Gemini has a creep problem.
A few years ago, that little sparkle icon started showing up in all of our Google apps. Gemini in your inbox! Gemini in your Google Drive! It was slow at first, and easy enough to tune out, but something has changed in the past few months. Gemini is creeping. It's showing up in all kinds of places at a relentless pace, and personally, it's starting to really cheese me off. 
The AI-everywhere fatigue is familiar to anyone who has ever used Windows 11. Microsoft went absolutely bananas putting Copilot shortcuts onto every surface it could find, to the extreme irritation of many users. Likewise, we will doubtlessly …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: Gemini有危险完全变成Copilot
zh.summary: 谷歌的Gemini AI在谷歌应用程序中日益普遍，与微软在Windows 11中的激进Copilot推出相比较，引发了用户对"AI无处不在"的疲劳。

title: Solar to dominate energy by 2035, but AI data centers will keep fossil fuels in business
summary: Solar panel costs are projected to drop 30% by 2035, solidifying solar's dominance in energy markets, though AI data center demands will sustain ongoing fossil fuel usage.
content: Costs for solar panels are expected to drop another 30% in the coming decade, helping the tech cement its lead in energy markets.

zh.title: 太阳能将在2035年前主导能源市场，但人工智能数据中心将继续维持化石燃料的使用
zh.summary: 根据预测，到2035年太阳能电池板成本将下降30%，巩固太阳能在能源市场中的主导地位，但人工智能数据中心的需求将维持对化石燃料的持续使用。

title: Theo Baker spent four years investigating Stanford. Before he leaves, here’s what he found.
summary: Theo Baker's four-year investigation into Stanford reveals findings about the startup ecosystem and funding accessibility. The research highlights a notable trend where young people find it easier to raise startup capital than secure internships.
content: "There's a common refrain among [young] people in this world that it's easier to raise money for a startup right now than to get an internship. Which is remarkable, right?"

zh.title: Theo Baker花了四年时间调查斯坦福大学。在他离开之前，以下是他的发现。
zh.summary: Theo Baker对斯坦福大学进行的四年调查揭示了关于创业生态系统和融资可及性的发现。该研究突出了一个显著趋势，即年轻人更容易筹集创业资本而非获得实习机会。

title: LG will release the first 1000Hz, 1080p gaming monitor this year
summary: LG has announced the UltraGear 25G590B, the first gaming monitor to combine a native 1000Hz refresh rate with 1080p resolution, targeted at esports competitors. Previous 1000Hz monitors were limited to 720p, making this breakthrough significant for gamers seeking both responsiveness and display clarity.
content:
If you just can't choose between refresh rate and resolution, LG's next gaming monitor could solve your problem, as the UltraGear 25G590B monitor is the first one announced that will be capable of a native 1000Hz refresh rate at 1,920 x 1,080 resolution. So far, the 1,000Hz models we've seen have only been capable of 720p at the most, but this 24.5-inch IPS display does not have that limitation, as the company says it's ready for esports competitors (whether they're in an FPS or Excel), who need maximum responsiveness.
We were already asking if anyone could feel the effect of higher refresh rates a few years ago when they hovered around 360 …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: LG今年将推出首款1000Hz、1080p游戏显示器
zh.summary: LG推出了UltraGear 25G590B，这是首款结合原生1000Hz刷新率和1080p分辨率的游戏显示器，面向电竞选手。以前的1000Hz显示器仅限于720p，这一突破对于寻求响应速度和显示清晰度的玩家来说意义重大。
